name: Monstera xanthospatha
aboutText: Monstera xanthospatha is an elusive and highly sought-after climbing aroid native to the humid montane forests of Colombia. It is closely allied to the M. obliqua complex, characterized by its slender, robust stems and distinctively delicate, fenestrated foliage. This species thrives in shaded, high-humidity understories, where it utilizes strong aerial roots to ascend trees in rich organic substrates. Collectors prize the plant for its refined perforation pattern and unique yellowish-green new growth, which sets it apart from its more common relatives.

fieldNotes.title: Yellow-Spathed Colombian Rarity
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Xanthospatha means 'yellow-spathed' — referencing the distinctive yellowish spathe of the flower. A Colombian Monstera at ultra rare status and upper price tier. The combination of limited Colombian range and genuine scarcity in cultivation makes this a meaningful acquisition for the serious Monstera collector. Colombia hosts remarkable Monstera diversity that has been inconsistently surveyed, particularly in the Pacific biogeographic region, and colombian endemic species like this one represent collecting gaps in most European collections.
